title.zh: 哪條邊界最關鍵？德國選舉中的隱形疆界效應
title.en: Which Border Matters Most? The Hidden Border Effect in German Elections

summary.zh:
德國選舉中，行政邊界如何影響投票結果？這項研究揭開了「隱形疆界」的影響力。傳統選舉分析聚焦於選區或邦的整體結果，但本研究創新地轉向「鄰近選區間的選舉差異」——就像把視角從「單一房間」拉遠至「牆壁的分界」。研究者發現，1990年德國統一前的東西德邊界，至今仍是選舉差異最明顯的分水嶺。這條歷史性的「分界線」，不僅是地理標記，更成為政治認同的隱形壁壘。

更令人驚訝的是，選區（constituencies）對候選人票數影響巨大，卻對政黨得票幾乎無關；相反，州界、行政區界等更高層級的邊界，才是政黨投票差異的關鍵推手。研究者採用改良版「蓋勒漢指數」量化邊界影響，結果顯示：東德與西德選民的偏好，即使在統一30多年後，仍因行政邊界持續分野。這揭示了政治地理的深層邏輯：行政區劃不僅是治理工具，更是塑造選民認同的隱形力量。
summary.en:
This study reimagines electoral analysis by shifting focus from territorial units to the boundaries between them. Rather than examining election outcomes within constituencies or states, researchers analyzed electoral differences between neighboring units, revealing how administrative borders act as invisible barriers that shape voting behavior. The findings highlight a striking divide along the former inner-German border, where historical East-West divisions continue to influence election results decades after reunification.

The research demonstrates that constituencies significantly impact candidate votes but have minimal effect on party votes. In contrast, state, district, and county boundaries emerge as critical factors for party-level voting differences. Using a modified version of the Gallagher index, the study quantifies how electoral patterns diverge at these administrative borders, with the inner-German border showing particularly pronounced effects. This suggests that political geography is not merely a product of current governance structures but is deeply rooted in historical and administrative legacies that persist in shaping voter behavior.

keyArticle.title: Which border matters most? An analysis of how different types of internal boundaries affect voting behavior
